title: Reading - Part 1
instructions: For each question, choose the correct answer.

questions.heading: Young Writers Competition
questions.text: Write a story about an unusual journey and send it to the school office by 3 May. Stories must be your own work and no longer than two pages. Winners will read their stories at assembly.
questions.question: What does the poster say about the competition?

options.A: Students must follow limits for topic and length.
options.B: Students should write stories during assembly.
options.C: Winners will receive help writing their stories.

questions.explanation: The story must be about a journey and no longer than two pages.

questions.heading: Message from Sara to Will
questions.text: Will, I can’t find my red folder, and I think I left it in the art room. If you’re passing there before maths, could you check? Don’t worry if you’re already late.
questions.question: Why is Sara texting Will?

options.A: To ask for help if it is convenient.
options.B: To tell him he is late for maths.
options.C: To ask him to bring art materials.

questions.explanation: Sara asks Will to check only if he is passing the art room.

questions.heading: Community Garden
questions.text: Visitors may pick herbs from the boxes near the gate, but please leave the vegetables for our cooking classes. Children are welcome, although they must stay on the paths to protect new plants.
questions.question: What are visitors allowed to do?

options.A: Take some plants from one area.
options.B: Pick vegetables for cooking classes there.
options.C: Let children play anywhere in the garden.

questions.explanation: Visitors may pick herbs from boxes near the gate.

questions.heading: From: Priya / Subject: Your presentation
questions.text: Your slides look clear, and the pictures are useful. However, I think you should practise speaking more slowly. Yesterday some people couldn’t write notes because you moved through the information too quickly.
questions.question: What advice does Priya give?

options.A: Take more time when giving the talk.
options.B: Add clearer pictures to the slides.
options.C: Give people fewer notes to write.

questions.explanation: Priya says the speaker should practise speaking more slowly.

questions.heading: Bookshop Reading Club
questions.text: Join our reading club for young people aged 13 to 16. We meet once a month to discuss a book chosen by members. You can join even if you have not finished this month’s book.
questions.question: What does the advert say?

options.A: Members help decide what the group reads.
options.B: Members must read a new book every week.
options.C: Only people who finish books may attend.

questions.explanation: The book is chosen by members, and unfinished readers can still join.
